# Copilot Instructions — pcf-syntax-highlighter

## What this is

A **PowerApps PCF (PowerApps Component Framework) control** that renders syntax-highlighted code using [Shiki](https://shiki.matsu.io) — the same engine powering VS Code. It runs inside Power Apps and Power Pages as a standard control, with no external runtime dependencies (Shiki is bundled at build time).

Tech stack: **TypeScript · React · Shiki · PCF Standard Control**

---

## Commands

All commands run from `src/SyntaxHighlighter/`:

```bash
npm install          # Install dependencies
npm start watch      # Dev: opens PCF test harness at http://localhost:8181
npm run build        # Production build
```

There is no automated test suite — manual testing is done in the PCF test harness.

---

## Project structure

```
src/SyntaxHighlighter/
  SyntaxHighlighter.pcfproj           # MSBuild project file for solution packaging
  pcfconfig.json                       # PAC CLI config (namespace, name)
  package.json / tsconfig.json
  eslint.config.mjs
  SyntaxHighlighter/                   # Control source root
    ControlManifest.Input.xml          # PCF property & resource declarations (source of truth for props)
    index.ts                           # Control lifecycle: init / updateView / destroy
    HelloWorld.tsx                     # Starter React component — replace with SyntaxHighlighterComponent.tsx
    generated/
      ManifestTypes.d.ts               # Auto-generated by pac pcf init — do not edit manually
```

---

## Architecture

### PCF lifecycle → React

`index.ts` implements the PCF `StandardControl` interface. On `init` it creates the React root; on `updateView` it re-renders with updated props from `context.parameters`. All rendering logic lives in `SyntaxHighlighterComponent.tsx`.

### Shiki initialisation & caching

The Shiki highlighter instance is created **once at module level** and shared across all control instances on the page. Themes and languages are loaded lazily on first use (only what is actually configured is downloaded). Do not create a new highlighter per `updateView` call.

### Fallback on error

If Shiki fails to resolve a language or theme (e.g. a typo in a property value), the component falls back to a plain `<pre><code>` block and logs to `console.error`. Preserve this pattern when adding new Shiki calls.

---

## PCF-specific conventions

- **`ControlManifest.Input.xml`** is the canonical source for all control properties. Property names, types, and defaults declared there map directly to `context.parameters` in TypeScript.
- **`ManifestTypes.d.ts`** is auto-generated — regenerate with `pac pcf init` after manifest changes, never edit by hand.
- Property types follow PCF conventions: `Multiple` (bound text), `SingleLine.Text`, `TwoOptions`, `Whole.None`.
- The control targets **Standard Control** mode (not dataset), so `context.parameters` carries individual property values, not records.

---

## Control properties

| Property | PCF type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `code` | `Multiple` (bound) | — | Required. Bound to a text column. |
| `language` | `SingleLine.Text` | `typescript` | Any Shiki language ID |
| `theme` | `SingleLine.Text` | `github-dark` | Any Shiki theme name |
| `showLineNumbers` | `TwoOptions` | `true` | |
| `showCopyButton` | `TwoOptions` | `true` | |
| `headerLabel` | `SingleLine.Text` | `""` | Falls back to language name when empty |
| `fontSize` | `Whole.None` | `14` | px |

---

## Fonts

The control uses a local monospace font stack — no web fonts are loaded:

```
'Cascadia Code', 'Fira Code', 'JetBrains Mono', 'Consolas', monospace
```

Keep this offline-capable by not introducing CDN font loading unless the `fontSize` property or a future font property explicitly drives it.

---

## Deployment

```bash
pac solution init --publisher-name <name> --publisher-prefix <prefix>
pac solution add-reference --path .
msbuild /t:build /restore
pac solution import --path ./bin/Debug/<SolutionName>.zip
```
